title: File Transfer Protocol (FTP)
overview: File Transfer Protocol (FTP) is a standard application-layer protocol used for transferring files between hosts on a TCP/IP network. It was designed for reliability and efficiency, especially across systems with differing file systems. FTP operates over TCP port 21 for control and establishes a separate data connection for file transfers.

importantCommands.code:
$ ftp 10.0.0.10
Connected to 10.0.0.10.
220 (vsFTPd 3.0.5)
Name: anonymous
331 Please specify the password.
Password:
230 Login successful.
ftp> ls
227 Entering Passive Mode (10,0,0,10,215,90).
150 Here comes the directory listing.
-rw-r--r-- 1 0 0 1024 Jan 01 12:00 report.txt
226 Directory send OK.
ftp> type ascii
200 Switching to ASCII mode.
ftp> get report.txt
150 Opening ASCII mode data connection.
226 Transfer complete.
ftp> quit
221 Goodbye.

tips: - Data Channel: TCP/20 (Active Mode) or random high port (Passive Mode)
